blocks.content: The instinctive thought was to get out of there.
To activate the Diamond Armor, he needed to grasp the sword. But Armand's hilt was too far away.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Direct Control...
Armand, responding to Zion's mental wave, began to slide out of its sheath with a watery sound.
But before it could reach Zion's hand, Uorin swiftly grabbed the hilt with her left hand.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Zion glared at Uorin with hostility.
This wasn't the sister he knew. Not only had she grabbed Armand's hilt without looking, but she was also holding the sword in place with a strength that defied explanation.
Uorin slowly moved the arm holding Armand in a reverse grip.
Zion commanded the sword again, but Armand only vibrated in place, unable to break free.
As the blade approached Zion's neck, the vibration grew louder.
Uorin's wrist, visible outside her sleeve, was as slender as that of an ordinary girl.
He had never heard of her training in swordsmanship.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: How does she have this strength?
Uorin's face remained calm, showing no sign of exertion.
But in her mind, a powerful force of will was at work. In heaven, this technique was called Incarnation. In this world, swordsmen referred to it as Divine Transcendence.
Uorin slowly moved the sword toward Zion's neck.
Even with Divine Transcendence, it would be ineffective without physical support, but Zion's weak mental strength posed no problem.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: You are not worthy to be the master of this sword.
As Armand moved against his will, Zion's mind grew hazy.
The blade touched his neck.
The vibration intensified, cutting into his skin.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Cancel the contract. Otherwise, your head will be severed.
The blade felt icy cold where it touched, then suddenly burned like fire, making him scream involuntarily.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Cancel the contract!
At Zion's shout, Armand's vibration ceased.
Only then did Uorin release Zion's neck and sheath Armand.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: This sword is too precious for you. I'll put it to good use.
Zion, slumped against the wall, rubbed his neck where her hand had left a mark.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Who... who are you?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: You would do well to heed my words, mortal.
Leaving those words behind, Uorin exited the cell.
The door was open, but Zion couldn't move.
Only after she disappeared did he realize the reality of his situation.
He must do nothing. Until she released him, he would rot in the dungeon forever.
Overwhelmed by despair, tears streamed down his face.
He slowly collapsed to the side, curling up in a corner and trembling.
Uorin sheathed Armand and left the dungeon.
The combat-ready silver sheath had been custom-made by a craftsman.
Gold was the most stylish, but she had excluded it for practical reasons.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: I hope he likes it.
As she exited the castle gate, all traces of Teraje disappeared from Uorin's face.
She reverted to a fresh-faced 14-year-old girl, smiling brightly as she ran toward the carriage.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Shirone!
Shirone, who was loading luggage onto the carriage, turned his head.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Uorin was waving as she approached.
For a moment, a nerve in his brain seemed to pass through a certain point, but it was just an empty space.
After blinking a few times, he smiled lightly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Are you leaving now?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Yes. Thank you for everything. I felt bad about leaving without saying goodbye.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hehe, no need for formalities between us. We'll see each other again, right?
Shirone scratched his eyebrow awkwardly.
He had been in critical condition until recently, but fortunately, his mind was mostly intact.
If he continued his life as a mage, Uorin would be an unavoidable presence.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Are you still upset with me?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: It's not that. But we're too different.
Uorin smiled bitterly.
Anyone who had experienced what he did at the castle would feel the same.
But hearing it from someone she had hoped would be her future husband was disheartening.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Still... you consider me a friend, right?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Of course. No, you're my savior. You saved my life.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then that's enough. Don't worry. I won't bother you again.
Shirone realized the futility of words.
Uorin had taught him that.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Take care.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Oh! And this...
Uorin handed him the sword she had been carrying.
It was unmistakably Armand.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's unmistakably Armand.
Shirone stared at it, puzzled, as Uorin explained.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: I'm giving it to you as a gift. Zion won't be out of the dungeon for a long time.
He had heard from Reina that Zion was alive.
He didn't know the details, but Teraje had personally imprisoned him for disgracing their bloodline.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I don't know if I should accept it. I'm not a swordsman. It should go to someone who needs it more.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Hehe, you don't know Armand's true value yet. Just make a contract. If you don't like it, you can always cancel.
Shirone finally took Armand.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So, how do I do this?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Hold the sword with both hands, stand it vertically, and say, 'I, the great mage Shirone, command Armand to obey me.'
Shirone looked at Uorin incredulously.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Just say 'Contract.'
Shirone drew Armand and said, 'Contract.'
The jewel glowed, and a majestic vibration spread through his palm.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is the contract done? I don't feel anything.
He knew that shared consciousness with its user.
But he felt no different than before.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: It's because you haven't activated Armand yet. Direct Control requires copying the user's brain with Diamond Armor. Say 'Diamond Armor.'
Amy, who had seen Diamond Armor in action, swallowed nervously.
The blade opened into a skeletal frame, enveloping Shirone's face.
-Scanning new user's brain. Analyzing. Specializing in mental activity.
A grand voice echoed in his mind, as if transmitted through a golden crown.
The frame tightened around his body, and organic material flowed out.

-Activating neural network. Detecting focal points. Relaxing beta waves. Maximizing theta waves. Searching for time division sections. Usable. Suggesting materialization. Approved. Creating exobrain. Searching for conceptual term. External.
A pressure was felt on the leather of his right gauntlet.
Looking at his palm, he saw a convex glass hemisphere embedded there.
Light emanated from it, forming a glassy substance in the air.
A completed amethyst orb quickly circled around Shirone.
It was an external brain, specialized for his time division ability.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wow...
Everyone watching Shirone was wide-eyed.
With the hood on, Shirone looked like a perfect professional mage.
The organic material forming the robe was desert-colored, not flashy, but it made him look stronger.
Unaware of his changed appearance, Shirone was amazed by the internal changes.
His physical abilities had increased, but his mind felt refreshingly clear.
It was at least twice as invigorating as his best condition.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is incredible...
Uorin nodded with a satisfied smile.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Armand enhances the user's strengths and compensates for weaknesses.
Now deactivate Diamond Armor and try Direct Control.
When Shirone thought of deactivating, the Diamond Armor disassembled.
Armand, now a sleek sword, flew through the air at Shirone's command.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thank you. But no matter how I think about it, this is too much. It's an S-class object.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Oh, brother, you really are something. But that's why I like you.
Uorin tested him slightly, but Shirone didn't seem to catch any special meaning.
Maybe it's too soon? But someday...
She thought, as she held Shirone's arm to ease his burden.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: It's okay. Objects like this need to meet the right owner to be truly effective. Please take good care of it. See you next time.
With that, Uorin turned and walked away.
She knew Shirone's personality well enough to understand that if she didn't leave decisively, he wouldn't be able to depart for hours.
Shirone felt grateful for Uorin's consideration and got into the carriage.
As the horses neighed and the carriage started moving, Shirone's group cheered.
Going home felt like a dream.
For a while, the carriage was lively with chatter.
They gossiped about the Kazra nobles and speculated about .
After an hour, the carriage, furnished like a home, fell into a peaceful silence.
ΓÇö
